title: Repeat audit findings — same control deficiency across consecutive audit cycles
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate root cause; surfaces the pattern for management attention.
evidence: Quarterly 'repeat_findings_report' writes repeat deficiencies to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Repeat findings are an indicator of material weakness; this UC surfaces the early pattern.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of SOX ITGC ITGC.Logging.Review (Log review) — Splunk UC-22.47.2: Repeat audit findings — same control deficiency across consecutive audit cycles.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.47.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC3.1 risk assessment — repeat findings indicate unresolved risk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 CC3.1 (Risk assessment) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.47.2: Repeat audit findings — same control deficiency across consecutive audit cycles.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.47.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When the same control (control_id) has an open deficiency in two consecutive audit cycles, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Controls whose deficiencies were closed between cycles do NOT fire.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=audit:finding earliest=-2y
| stats dc(cycle_id) as cycles, values(cycle_id) as cycle_ids by control_id
| where cycles>=2
| table control_id cycles cycle_ids
description: Surfaces controls whose deficiencies persist across audit cycles — the canonical trigger for escalation to the audit committee.
value: Converts the 'prior year issue' anecdote into a quantitative signal.
implementation: (1) Ingest audit findings per cycle; (2) schedule quarterly; (3) escalate to audit committee. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=audit:finding`.
visualization: Bar chart of repeat controls, heatmap of control × cycle, single-value 'repeat findings count'. Key fields: `control_id`, `cycle_ids`, `cycles`.
knownFalsePositives: Evidence stale dates during quarterly cycles, control owner transitions, or platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest audit findings per cycle; (2) schedule quarterly; (3) escalate to audit committee.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=audit:finding earliest=-2y
| stats dc(cycle_id) as cycles, values(cycle_id) as cycle_ids by control_id
| where cycles>=2
| table control_id cycles cycle_ids
```

Understanding this SPL

**Repeat audit findings — same control deficiency across consecutive audit cycles** — Converts the 'prior year issue' anecdote into a quantitative signal.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: audit:finding. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=audit:finding, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **control_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where cycles>=2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: control_id, cycle_ids, cycles.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart of repeat controls, heatmap of control × cycle, single-value 'repeat findings count'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when control evidence is getting old so you refresh proof before the next review.
